content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print the length of each word of the sentence given below as well as the length of the whole sentence.

**"Coding on CodeChef"**

[**Note:** Don't forget to print the outputs in same format as given below.]
constraints: S consists of printable ASCII characters and spaces
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a sentence S (may contain spaces).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the lengths of the words in S in order, each followed by a space, and finally the length of the whole sentence S (including spaces) followed by a newline.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The sentence has three words: Coding (6), on (2), CodeChef (8). Total characters including two spaces = 17.
Two words of length 5 each, plus one space gives total length 11.
Word lengths are 4,2,1,4 and total length including three spaces is 11.

solution.approach: For each test case we need two pieces of information: the length of every word and the total length of the original line (including spaces). The total length is simply S.length(). To obtain the word lengths we split the line on whitespace. In C/C++/Java we can use the standard tokenizers (strtok, stringstream, StringTokenizer) which automatically skip consecutive spaces, so empty tokens are ignored. For each token we output its length followed by a space. After processing all tokens we output the previously computed total length and a newline. The algorithm runs in O(|S|) time per test case and uses O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S|) per test case, O(total input size) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input string.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0].strip())
    out_lines = []
    for i in range(1, T+1):
        s = data[i]
        total_len = len(s)
        parts = [str(len(w)) for w in s.split()]
        parts.append(str(total_len))
        out_lines.append(' '.join(parts))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
